I know the story. It transpired after Charlie 'Lucky' Luciano had to step down as the head of the syndicate due to being imprisoned for 50 years, then eventually exiled from America. That's when the power struggle began. Luciano wanted Costello to take his place. His other two closest mob friends Bugsy Siegal* was a violent hot head and Mayer Lansky was not Sicilian or Italian, so it fell to Castello to take on the role as head of the family.

Genovese was a very dangerous individual who had worked loyally for Luciano, but he was a homicidal maniac, who would kill without hesitation. He felt overlooked and decided that Costello had to go so he could take over.

*Siegal was eventually killed by order of his own mob friends (Luciano and Lansky) for skimming money from the mob funded construction of the Flamingo hotel in Vegas. The first gambling casino hotel built there, still standing today.

*I WAS a lifelong De Niro fan, but now I can't stand the guy. I doubt I'll watch this movie due to that, as much as the story interests me*

Yknow it’s probably one of the better historically accurate movies based on historical accounts that have been documented. I will say the ending accounts given the the perspective of Frank were new to me. That he was the unknown caller that tipped off state police and that Albert Anastasia was more of a protector than the big baddie in this scenario was different definitely added more context. Not the grittiest film but given it is from the perspective of arguably the most gentlemanly boss in mafia history arguably it made sense that it wasn’t so violent in these years. I think if Vito Genovese had a stand alone film and Martin directed it and Joe Pesci or someone else played the role for Vito and the story focused on him and his life it would have been a bit more gritty like some expected given my knowledge that Vito was as they described in the movie are the accounts of him in history. I think De Niro played them both well I forgot it was De Niro after a little bit and hats off to the casting. Cosmo Jarvis cast as The Chin was awesome. Definitely could see Cosmo Jarvis recast as Sonny Corleone in a future Godfather prequel film or mini series. Didn’t see it until I saw his face shaven and the curly hair in this type of movie. Voice might be the only issue. And Michael Rispoli cast as Albert Anastasia was phenomenal. He was the best in the whole film. I literally thought you know who has yet to get a respectable film?! Albert Anastasia and Michael Rispoli cast as him was amazing. Like I thought to myself yknow he didn’t get his honest shake in Sopranos and I’ve seen him in some of his earlier films and his characters always leave me wanting more. 1.  Neither man would talk to the other like this during a sitdown.  
2.  Costello never was on Genovese's level in terms of respect in the streets and Genovese was one of the real founding fathers of La Costa Nostra.  He was main mobster who was doing most of the work when Maranzano was whacked. 
3. When Costello said the government runs things, at that time the mafia had way more power.  This was peak Tammany hall takeover in the government as the former politics from Tammany rose to high level positions like senators and representatives and judges on the state level.  
4.  Its important to note fact they had ties with US Senators is huge as essentially they could control the laws directly. Costello wasnt the only due with politican connections.  
5.  Many young mobsters in their late teens and early 20s formed friendships with Tammany hall employees.
6.  When Costello says her needs to find way to take it all down, I can almost promise you this was never said and Costello never fought back after being shot. 
7.  Genovese was a real shooter, he was one of if not the most feared member of the 5 families and almost nobody tried him including Costello.
8.  I would have much rather preferred Gambino instead of Costello and had the movie about those 2 rising to power as when they were bosses the mafia was at its peak, those 2 men just might be most influential bosses to the modern families after they died.
9.  Costello never could be the permanent boss of a family because the streets didnt respect him because he wasnt a shooter.
10.  At this period, most of the bosses were both money makers and dudes who did a lot work.  Most of the 5 families wanted Costello gone because he wasnt a real mafioso, the high ranking members legit ordered him to retire LOL.  Very rare in LCN.

As an educated organized crime historian and connoisseur of the gangster film genre, I can say that this trailer looks good! One of my favorite clients at my job Kevin McCormick worked on this film and we've been talking about it for the past year. 
I must say however that Barry Levinson's film Bugsy starring the great Warren Beatty was a bit of a disappointment! It came across as a lightweight romantic drama that involved some soft gangsters. 
Bill Graham as Lucky Luciano was horrible casting! So was Elliott Gould as the thug Harry Greenberg. Ben Kingsley as Meyer Lansky was solid, and Harvey Keitel as Mickey Cohen was the best!
I also think that Beatty was as solid as Ben Siegel physically, but, he wasn't convincing as a hitman from Brooklyn representing an outfit like Murder Incorporated!
I think that Annette Bening ruined the entire film, and Levinson's direction was lacking for a gangster film. 
Anyway, Alto Knights looks promising and Deniro is also looking solid as well! His makeup as Vito Genovese is very good. 
McCormick told me that the Albert Anastasia barbershop scene is great by the way!
